title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Delivery Case: Docker Platform Index Selection
summary: A CLI release must support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 without silently shipping an emulated, untested, or single-platform image. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker platform index selection increment in multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Platform Index Selection · Theory Docker Platform Index Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Platform Index Selection contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility. A defensible solution applies Docker Platform Index Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-5
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: worked docker multiplatform buildx bake 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A multi-platform tag points directly to one universal root filesystem. This fails the Docker Platform Index Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-platform-index-selection-f4vf0e
  - competency: docker-platform-index-selection
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: platform index selection
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: rendered Compose model and dependency health transition
    case-key: f4vf0e
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
